mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples; when policy is easing, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during falling-FEDFUNDS periods, or if sign accuracy stays below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which suppresses equity earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates weaker payroll income and retail spending, reducing near-term revenue expectations for index constituents and depressing S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal is not more negative than either series alone, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and operating leverage, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO periods do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC is persistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply, housing activity is contracting and equity investors anticipate weaker construction, consumer durables, and credit demand, which pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by non-negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is below 50% across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE captures a true labor-market downturn better than either series alone; when payroll growth slows and unemployment rises, S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction does not outperform single-variable versions in out-of-sample sign prediction, or if negative-return frequency is not elevated when payrolls slow and unemployment rises together.

mechanism_narrative: In tightening cycles, a high FEDFUNDS rate signals restrictive financing conditions that weigh on equity valuation and future cash flows, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the average return differential is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a demand-rebound regime, simultaneous acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates stronger final sales and revenue growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDP-sales interaction fails to improve 60-day return prediction versus either series alone, or if positive returns are not concentrated in periods where both series accelerate together.
